votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the sum of squares of positive odd integers using a loop, matching the Coq fold_left logic.
The Python code correctly implements the logic of summing the squares of positive odd integers from the list and comparing it to the result, exactly matching the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by summing the squares of all positive odd integers in the list and checking if it equals the given result.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. It iterates through the list, checking if each number is both odd (num % 2 != 0) and positive (num > 0), and if so, adds the square of that number to the accumulator. This matches the Coq fold_left with the condition (Z.odd num && (0 <? num))%bool. The function returns whether the given result equals the computed sum, which matches the Coq Prop 'result = fold_left ...'. For all concrete inputs, both will produce the same judgment.
